messages.content:
Describe what a buffer overflow is and how it relates to this vulnerability.

The APDFL.dll contains an out-of-bounds write past the fixed-length heap-based buffer while parsing specially crafted PDF files. This could allow an attacker to execute code in the context of the current process.

A buffer overflow occurs when more data is written to a buffer than it can hold, causing data to overwrite adjacent memory. In the context of this CVE, an out-of-bounds write involves writing outside the allocated heap buffer, which in turn can be manipulated by an attacker to execute arbitrary code.
